summary: Factoring rewrites a polynomial as a product of simpler factors. It is the key to solving equations and to spotting roots at a glance.
method: First pull out any common factor shared by every term.
Recognise special patterns — a difference of squares, or a perfect-square trinomial.
For a quadratic x² + bx + c, find two numbers that multiply to c and add to b.
Write the result as a product, and check by expanding that you recover the original.

example.title: Factor a quadratic
steps: Look for two numbers multiplying to 6 and adding to 5.
